aliases: The town of Our Lady the Queen of the Angels of the Little Portion
description: seat of Los Angeles County, and largest city in California, United States
description_wiki: Los Angeles (often referred to by its initials, LA) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of California, and the commercial, financial, and cultural center of Southern California.
Yamaguchi Prefecture (山口県, Yamaguchi-ken) is a prefecture of Japan located in the Chūgoku region of Honshu.

surface_forms: the Japanese vernacular newspaper, the Sangyo Nippo

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: a makeshift concentration camp set up by the U.S. Army in 1942 to incarcerate people of Japanese ancestry
description_wiki: The Temporary Detention Camp for Japanese Americans / Santa Anita Assembly Center is one of the places Japanese Americans were held during World War II. The Santa Anita Assembly Center was designated a California Historic Landmark (No.934.07) on May 13, 1980.

description: city in and county seat of Wayne County, and largest city in State of Michigan, United States
description_wiki: Detroit (  dih-TROYT, locally also  DEE-troyt) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Michigan.

description: city and county seat of Erie County, New York, United States
description_wiki: Buffalo is a city in the U.S. state of New York.

description: World War II internment camp for Japanese-Americans
description_wiki: The Rohwer War Relocation Center was a World War II Japanese American concentration camp located in rural southeastern Arkansas, in Desha County.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Arkansas ( , AR-kən-saw) is a landlocked state in the West South Central region of the Southern United States.

aliases: Girl Scouts of the United States of America
description_wiki: Girl Scouts of the United States of America (GSUSA), commonly referred to as Girl Scouts, is a youth organization for girls in the United States and American girls living abroad.

description: largest city of the state of Louisiana, United States
description_wiki: New Orleans (commonly known as NOLA or  The Big Easy, among other nicknames) is a consolidated city-parish located along the Mississippi River in the U.S. state of Louisiana.

description: movie theater and music venue in Los Angeles, California, United States
description_wiki: The Orpheum Theatre at 842 S. Broadway in Downtown Los Angeles opened on February 15, 1926, as the fourth and final Los Angeles venue for the Orpheum vaudeville circuit.

surface_forms: segregation and all that was still happening
aliases: history of racial segregation in the United States
description: race based restrictions in American society
description_wiki: Facilities and services such as housing, healthcare, education, employment, and transportation have been systematically separated in the United States based on racial categorizations.

surface_forms: Chinese man who was in the shrimp business
description_wiki: Chinese Americans are Americans of Chinese ancestry.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Louisiana (French: Louisiane; Spanish: Luisiana [lwiˈsjana]; Louisiana Creole: Lwizyàn) is a state in the Deep South and South Central regions of the United States.
